segments.text: Just the same, my complaints today remain bitter. In spite of my groaning he is still punishing me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If only I knew where I could find him so I could go to where he sits in judgment.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There I would lay out my case before him and present all my arguments in full.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I would discover how he would answer me and learn what he has to say to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Would he fight against me using his mighty power? No, he would pay attention to what I have to say.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: There a good person could reason with him, and I would be acquitted forever by my judge.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I go to the east, he's not there; if I go the west, I don't find him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If he's working in the north, I don't perceive him; if he's moving south, I don't see him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet he always knows where I'm going. When he has proved me, I will come out shining like gold.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have kept in step with him; I have followed his way without turning aside.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have not neglected his commands, for I value what he has told me to do more than the food I eat every day.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But God is unchangeable—who can turn him from his purposes? He does whatever he wants to do.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: So he will finish whatever he has planned for me—and he has many plans for me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: That's why I'm terrified at meeting him;  when I think of him I tremble with fear.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: God has made me faint-hearted; the Almighty has scared me to death.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet I'm still here despite the dark—even though I can't see through the utter darkness.
segments.speaker: Job
